hero.title: The impact capital allocation gap
hero.lede: Web3 startups with sophisticated products navigate local regulation with uncertainty; sustainability projects don't speak the allocator's language. Without institutional certainty, the cost of allocating capital spikes and shuts the investor out. ReFaz connects these worlds.
hero.statsLabel: Our solutions and the organizations we've contributed to have reached:
hero.caption: ReFaz · Brazil · 2025
diag.label: DIAGNOSIS
diag.title: Two blocked ecosystems, one root cause
diag.c1b: They can't demonstrate results in the language and format demanded by the rigid compliance structures of international capital.
diag.c2b: They navigate the risk of innovation inside a highly regulated environment and, lacking access to regulatory know-how, stay off the allocators' radar.
diag.c3b: They can't reach initiatives that generate real impact or startups with proven growth.
diag.callout: ROOT CAUSE — Information asymmetry raises the cost of trust and paralyzes capital allocation.

proj.heroQuote: Active contributions to the Web3 ecosystem.
proj.heroSub: Four integrated verticals tackling the core problem — information asymmetry between impact capital and regenerative projects.
proj.c1body: We turn socio-environmental impact projects into assets perfectly legible to the global asset manager. The ReFaz engine applies the ISSB / IFRS S1&S2 standards + ReFaz Certification + Blockchain Timestamping.
proj.balaioBody: Micro-digital marketplace + transparent budget execution for local economies. 100 active users.
proj.rastaBody: Blockchain infrastructure for Brazilian culture and the creative economy. 30 users in pre-launch.
proj.gbBody: Blockchain mechanisms for sustainable finance at the official National Treasury hackathon. An ISSB-aligned automation system for green bonds.
proj.ekBody: A blockchain-native platform for verifiable socio-environmental data. Selected by the innovation lab of the Central Bank of Brazil.
proj.bneBody: From zero blockchain knowledge to hackathon winner. The program combines gamified learning with hands-on use of digital wallets, NFTs, and DAOs in public schools and universities.

mc.heroSub: Balaio is already an onchain escrow engine that pays for results. The same mechanic that coordinates tasks and releases funds on approval serves, with nothing rewritten, as a rail for credit issuance and settlement.
mc.s1title: Two walls: cost of operation and cost of trust
mc.s1sub: Lending R$ 200 for a delivery worker to buy a backpack doesn't cover the structure a bank needs to lend R$ 200,000. And those at the edge have no way to prove backing and collateral in the language capital demands.
mc.s1body: The result is familiar: smaller-ticket productive credit simply doesn't happen — or happens informally, expensively, and without a trace.
mc.s2title: A product in production, not a prototype
mc.c1body: Funds locked in onchain escrow, released only on validated delivery. The smart contract is the custodian — no intermediary.
mc.c2body: Runs on Celo and Gnosis, accepting CRC and integrated with the Circles wallet. Identity via GoodDollar.
mc.partners: Partnerships with GoodDollar, Green Pill Brasil, Blockchain na Escola, and UNIFACS.
mc.s3title: The task as a credit primitive
mc.s3sub: A credit operation is, structurally, identical to a task with escrow. The operation is born as a task — created by the lender (supply) or the borrower (demand) — describing the terms. When there is collateral, it is locked in a private task and the TaskID is handed over. The contract does the rest — no new line of code, just a new reading of what already exists.
mc.tblR2: Collateral optional, locked in a private task when the parties agree
mc.tblR3: Submission as task completion (statement, income, or crypto collateral)
mc.tblR5: Settlement via the rail; method and term private between the parties
mc.s4sub: Organizations and communities that want to run their own credit micro-ecosystem access the white-label page, register the organization (KYC), upload branding and logo — the environment becomes their own.
mc.c3body: The organization attaches documents proving authorization to issue credit. Onchain audit trail by default.
mc.c4body: Agents interact directly with the smart contract. Used as a sandbox for schools and finance educators running experiments with their own tokens — not a commercial product.
mc.s5sub: The same rail serves two commercial fronts, distinguished by the risk reducer that makes the credit viable.
mc.p1body: Finances the expansion of the businesses that serve a base of 150,000 people. Real collateral, strict compliance — a regulatable, licensable asset. Risk reducer: collateral.
mc.p2body: Credit to already-incubated impact initiatives, with personalized analysis from proprietary origination data. Risk reducer: data.
mc.pnote: Conservative, illustrative projections anchored in market references — microcredit default rates (BACEN) and a conservative rate of ~4%/month. They do not constitute a promise of return.
mc.s6title: The allocator is the client, not the community
mc.s6sub: Balaio doesn't charge the community. The product is qualified origination, ready compliance, and auditable settlement — delivered to whoever allocates capital.
mc.r1body: 3% on creation and 2% on claim, built into the contract. They cover the infrastructure — they are not the margin.
mc.r2body: A fraction of the yield, only on operations that settle. Fully aligned with whoever allocates.
mc.r3body: The more allocators share a curated pool, the lower the rail cost per allocator.
mc.c5body: The same model as platforms like Decentral — end-to-end origination and structuring — applied to productive impact credit. Every operation generates revenue at origination; default stays external to the rail. Balaio is the meeting point between those who need capital and those who offer it, charging for the infrastructure, not the risk.
mc.s7title: Co-design the first pool with us
mc.s7sub: We invite aligned allocators to design the first pool — terms defined in advance and tied to the target audience. You access a qualified, filtered audience with ready compliance.
mc.c6title: Get the communities onto the rail
mc.c6body: The two communities (160k people) running productive credit on Balaio.
mc.c7body: Security in credit issuance, with ReFaz's regulatory know-how in the BACEN / CVM context.
mc.c8body: Harden the flow for multi-organization operation.
mc.s9title: Scale revenue comes from licensing, not from each operation
mc.s9sub: Each community or ecosystem becomes a curated, licensed pool on the same already-proven rail. Scale comes from replicating pools and licensing the infrastructure — not from charging per transaction.
mc.s8title: The opportunity off traditional credit's radar
mc.s8sub: Brazil's crypto framework has matured, microcredit remains dammed by the cost of operation, and no one has joined the two ends with a product that's already running. ReFaz brings what can't be replicated: a rail in production, networks already under contract, and the regulatory-adequacy layer that turns a good credit idea into an asset legible to capital.
